description: Rules for pairing bean attributes (process, roast level, tasting scores) with brewing methods to suggest intelligent starting parameters. These modifiers stack on top of baseline recipes and roast level modifiers.

pour_over.notes: Washed coffees extract cleanly and predictably; standard baseline parameters work best.

french_press.notes: Standard extraction works perfectly for clean washed beans in immersion.

aeropress.notes: Washed beans behave predictably under pressure; use the baseline recipe.

moka_pot.notes: Baseline moka pot parameters suit washed coffees.

cold_brew.notes: Standard steeping time and grind size are optimal for clear extraction.

south_indian_filter.notes: Standard decoction parameters work efficiently for washed Indian coffees.

pour_over.notes: Natural processed coffees extract surface sugars quickly; a slightly coarser grind prevents over-extraction and fermented harshness, though fast water flow mitigates some risk.

french_press.notes: Long immersion times can pull harsh, over-fermented notes from naturals; start slightly coarser to control extraction speed.

aeropress.notes: Immersion combined with pressure extracts naturals rapidly; grinding coarser keeps flavors clean and bright.

moka_pot.notes: High heat and pressure easily over-extract the complex sugars in naturals; start coarser.

cold_brew.notes: Long steeps can highlight fermented, boozy notes in naturals; a coarser grind mitigates this over-extraction.

south_indian_filter.notes: Natural beans will over-extract in the slow South Indian gravity drip; grind slightly coarser than usual.

pour_over.notes: Honey process is generally forgiving; baseline parameters provide a good starting point.

french_press.notes: Maintains good sweetness and body at baseline settings without becoming harsh.

aeropress.notes: Baseline recipe works well, extracting the added sweetness smoothly.

moka_pot.notes: Honey process handles moka pot heat well; baseline parameters usually suffice unless it's a black honey.

cold_brew.notes: Extracts sweetly with standard cold brew parameters.

south_indian_filter.notes: Produces a very sweet decoction; baseline works well.

pour_over.notes: Extremely porous structure means water absorbs rapidly; treat like one roast level darker than labeled.

french_press.notes: Readily gives up soluble material in immersion; lowering temp and grinding coarser prevents bitterness.

aeropress.notes: Extracts intensely under pressure; cool the water slightly and grind coarser to balance the heavy body.

moka_pot.notes: Monsooned beans extract very fast under intense pressure; use a coarser grind to slow it down.

cold_brew.notes: The high solubility of monsooned beans means they over-extract quickly even in cold water; grind coarser.

south_indian_filter.notes: A classic pairing, but its spongy, porous nature requires a slightly coarser grind and cooler water to avoid harshness.

light.adjustment: If user wants more body, the bean may not deliver it through this method. Suggest ratio decrease (more coffee) OR suggest french_press/south_indian_filter which naturally produce heavier body.

heavy.adjustment: Great for immersion methods. For pour_over, may taste muddy — suggest slightly coarser grind.

low.adjustment: Don't push extraction hard looking for brightness — it's not in the bean. Focus on sweetness and body instead. Lower the user's expectations for clarity/brightness goals.

high.adjustment: Great for pour_over. For french_press/south_indian_filter, the long contact time may make it overly sharp — suggest slightly coarser grind.

low.adjustment: The 'more sweetness' improvement goal will have limited returns. Flag this to the user: 'This bean has naturally low sweetness — we can optimise extraction but sweetness ceiling is limited.'

high.adjustment: Target the 18-22% extraction sweet spot more aggressively — the bean has sweetness to unlock.

monsooned.condition: is_monsooned is true OR process is monsooned

blend_handling.behavior: Process adjustments don't apply cleanly (blend may mix processes). Fall back to roast level only.
blend_handling.warning_message: This is a blend — process-specific adjustments are less reliable. Using roast-level defaults.
